title: Trailing whitespace trim in json_find_value_b is dead code
description: In json_find_value_b, after finding the matching key, there's a trailing-whitespace trim on the value slice: rposition of non-whitespace bytes. But skip_value_b always returns the byte position immediately after the last byte of the value token — no trailing whitespace is ever included in [value_start..value_end]. The trim is dead code. The test navigate_whitespace_around_value tests that *leading* whitespace is stripped (done by skip_ws_b before value_start), not trailing. The dead trim plus the test name create a false impression that trailing whitespace is a real concern here. Remove the trim code; it confuses maintainers.
